ucs.title: [LLM] APT18 (Dynamite Panda) pisloader DNS TXT-record C2 beaconing
ucs.description: Hunts for the DNS TXT-based command-and-control channel characteristic of APT18's pisloader/HTTPBrowser implants — high-frequency outbound DNS TXT queries from non-resolver processes to long, low-popularity subdomains, which is how Dynamite Panda exfiltrates data and receives commands while blending with legitimate DNS.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.app) as app dc(All_Traffic.dest) as dc_dest from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=53 All_Traffic.transport=udp NOT (All_Traffic.dest IN ("8.8.8.8","8.8.4.4","1.1.1.1","9.9.9.9")) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.process_id _time span=10m | rename All_Traffic.* as * | where count>=50 | eventstats avg(count) as avg_q stdev(count) as sd_q by src | where count > (avg_q + (2*sd_q)) | join type=left src process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.process) as process_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where NOT Processes.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","firefox.exe","msedge.exe","svchost.exe","dns.exe","nslookup.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_id | rename Processes.dest as src Processes.process_id as process_id] | where isnotnull(process_name) | table firstTime lastTime src process_name process_cmd dest count dc_dest
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 24h;
let dns_beacon = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where RemotePort == 53 and Protocol == "Udp"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("svchost.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","dns.exe","nslookup.exe")
| summarize qcount=count(), distinct_dst=dcount(RemoteIP), first=min(Timestamp), last=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, bin(Timestamp, 10m)
| where qcount >= 50
| extend duration_min = datetime_diff("minute", last, first)
| where duration_min >= 5;
let odd_dns_proc = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","powershell.exe") and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe")
| project DeviceId, suspicious_parent=InitiatingProcessFileName, suspicious_child=FileName, ProcessCommandLine, suspicious_time=Timestamp;
dns_beacon
| join kind=leftouter (odd_dns_proc) on DeviceId
| project first, last, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, qcount, distinct_dst, duration_min, suspicious_parent, suspicious_child, ProcessCommandLine
| order by qcount desc
ucs.rationale: APT18/Dynamite Panda's pisloader family (publicly documented by Palo Alto Unit 42 and Dell SecureWorks as TG-0416) is one of the few APT tools that uses DNS TXT records as its primary C2 channel. The combination of (a) a non-resolver process generating sustained DNS UDP/53 traffic, (b) a Microsoft Office or scripting parent spawning the network-emitting child, and (c) high query volume to a small

ucs.title: [LLM] APT18 legacy 'at.exe' scheduled-task lateral movement chain
ucs.description: Detects APT18's distinctive use of the deprecated 'at' command to schedule cmd.exe payloads on remote hosts — a tradecraft fingerprint documented by Dell SecureWorks for TG-0416 that persists even though Windows has long superseded 'at' with schtasks, making it a low-volume, high-fidelity signal.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="at.exe" OR (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process="*at \\\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | rename Processes.* as * | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Authentication.src) as logon_src values(Authentication.user) as logon_user from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.authentication_method=NTLM earliest=-15m by Authentication.dest | rename Authentication.dest as dest] | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as deleted_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=deleted Filesystem.file_path="*\\Windows\\Tasks\\*" earliest=-1h by Filesystem.dest | rename Filesystem.dest as dest] | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent process_name cmdline logon_src logon_user deleted_files
ucs.kql:
let at_usage = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "at.exe" or (FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\bat\s+\\\\")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\\\", "/sc", "cmd", ".bat", ".exe")
| project AtTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, AtCmd=ProcessCommandLine, Parent=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let remote_logon = IdentityLogonEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "LogonSuccess" and Protocol in ("NTLM","Kerberos")
| project LogonTime=Timestamp, DeviceName=DestinationDeviceName, RemoteSrc=IPAddress, LogonUser=AccountName;
let task_cleanup = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileDeleted" and FolderPath has_any (@"\Windows\Tasks\", @"\Windows\System32\Tasks\")
| project DelTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeletedFile=FileName, DelInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName;
at_usage
| join kind=leftouter (remote_logon) on DeviceName
| where AtTime between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + 30m) or isempty(LogonTime)
| join kind=leftouter (task_cleanup) on DeviceId
| where DelTime between (AtTime .. AtTime + 1h) or isempty(DelTime)
| project AtTime, DeviceName, AccountName, AtCmd, Parent, ParentCmd, RemoteSrc, LogonUser, DeletedFile, DelInitiator
| order by AtTime desc
ucs.rationale: Modern admin tooling and Microsoft GPO have effectively retired 'at.exe' (it's deprecated since Windows 8 and removed from many builds), so legitimate sightings are exceedingly rare. APT18 is specifically documented (Dell SecureWorks 'Lateral Movement' research on TG-0416) chaining valid-account NTLM logons + 'at \\HOST time cmd /c ...' + post-execution deletion of the task file under \Windows\Tas
